item.title: 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme for Young Diplomats: Fully Immersive International Relations Training in Austria and Poland
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Young diplomats and emerging international relations professionals from the OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation countries now have an exceptional opportunity to gain firsthand experience within one of the world’s most influential security and co-operation organizations. The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE), through its External Co-operation Section, has officially launched the […]

The post 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme for Young Diplomats: Fully Immersive International Relations Training in Austria and Poland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young diplomats and emerging international relations professionals from the OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation countries now have an exceptional opportunity to gain firsthand experience within one of the world’s most influential security and co-operation organizations.

The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE), through its External Co-operation Section, has officially launched the call for nominations for the 2026 Study Visit Programme for OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation Young Diplomats. This prestigious two-week programme will take place from 28 September to 9 October 2026 and will provide selected participants with unique exposure to the OSCE’s work, institutions, and decision-making processes.

The programme will be hosted across two major European cities—Vienna, Austria, where the OSCE Secretariat is headquartered, and Warsaw, Poland, home to the OSCE Office for Democratic Institutions and Human Rights (ODIHR).

With only eight participants selected for the programme, this highly competitive opportunity offers direct engagement with international diplomacy, multilateral co-operation, democratic governance initiatives, human rights institutions, media freedom mechanisms, and regional security frameworks.

About the 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme

The OSCE Study Visit Programme is the flagship initiative of the OSCE Secretariat’s External Co-operation Section designed to strengthen engagement between the OSCE and its Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation.

The programme provides young diplomats and international affairs professionals with a comprehensive understanding of the OSCE’s role in promoting:

  • Regional security
  • Conflict prevention
  • Democratic governance
  • Human rights protection
  • Rule of law
  • Media freedom
  • International co-operation

Participants will spend two weeks engaging directly with OSCE experts, policymakers, and institutional representatives through a combination of:

  • Briefings
  • Presentations
  • Interactive discussions
  • Institutional visits
  • Networking opportunities
  • Participation in OSCE-related events

The programme offers a rare behind-the-scenes look at how one of the world’s largest regional security organizations operates.

Programme Dates and Locations

Programme Period

28 September – 9 October 2026

Host Locations

  • Vienna, Austria
  • Warsaw, Poland

The inclusion of Warsaw as part of the 2026 programme represents an important component of the study visit, allowing participants to engage directly with institutions and stakeholders connected to ODIHR in its host country.

What Participants Will Learn

Selected participants will gain extensive exposure to the OSCE’s work and operational structures.

Key learning areas include:

1. Understanding the OSCE’s Mandate

Participants will explore:

  • The history and evolution of the OSCE
  • Regional security challenges
  • Conflict prevention strategies
  • International diplomacy mechanisms
  • Multilateral decision-making processes
  • Co-operative security frameworks

2. Exposure to OSCE Executive Structures

Participants will have the opportunity to visit and interact with various OSCE institutions and executive structures, gaining practical insights into their mandates and operations.

3. Human Rights and Democratic Governance

Through engagements with specialized OSCE bodies, participants will deepen their understanding of:

  • Human rights promotion
  • Democratic institution building
  • Election observation
  • Rule of law initiatives
  • Good governance practices

4. Freedom of Expression and Media Freedom

The programme will also introduce participants to OSCE efforts related to:

  • Media freedom
  • Protection of journalists
  • Freedom of expression
  • Information integrity
  • Democratic public discourse

5. Parliamentary Engagement

Participants will learn how parliamentary diplomacy contributes to international co-operation through interactions with representatives and offices connected to the OSCE Parliamentary Assembly.

Institutions Participants Will Visit

One of the most valuable aspects of the programme is direct exposure to several key OSCE institutions.

These include:

OSCE Secretariat

The central administrative body supporting the OSCE’s activities and operations across participating states and partner countries.

OSCE Office for Democratic Institutions and Human Rights (ODIHR)

Participants will learn about ODIHR’s work in:

  • Human rights
  • Election observation
  • Democratic institution development
  • Rule of law promotion

OSCE Representative on Freedom of the Media (RFoM)

This institution focuses on:

  • Monitoring media freedom
  • Promoting freedom of expression
  • Supporting independent journalism
  • Addressing media-related challenges

OSCE Parliamentary Assembly (OSCEPA) Vienna Office

Participants will gain insight into the parliamentary dimension of OSCE activities and international diplomacy.

Benefits of Participating in the Programme

The programme offers numerous professional and academic advantages, including:

Professional Development

  • Exposure to international diplomacy
  • Understanding multilateral institutions
  • Enhanced policy knowledge
  • Increased understanding of regional security issues

Networking Opportunities

Participants will be able to connect with:

  • OSCE officials
  • International experts
  • Policymakers
  • Fellow young diplomats
  • International affairs professionals

Career Advancement

The programme can significantly strengthen future career prospects in:

  • Foreign affairs ministries
  • International organizations
  • Diplomatic services
  • Public policy institutions
  • Research organizations
  • Multilateral agencies

Practical Learning Experience

Participants will move beyond theoretical knowledge and gain firsthand experience of how international organizations operate in practice.

Eligibility Criteria

Delegations nominating candidates must ensure applicants meet all programme requirements.

Candidates must:

  1. Be under the age of 35.
  2. Have completed a first-level degree from an accredited university.
  3. Demonstrate professional fluency in English, both written and spoken.
  4. Possess prior work experience in international relations, preferably in a multilateral context.

Meeting the eligibility requirements does not guarantee selection, as the programme is highly competitive and limited to eight participants.

Application Process

For Prospective Candidates

Interested applicants should:

  1. Contact their relevant OSCE Partner for Co-operation delegation.
  2. Express interest in being nominated.
  3. Complete the required application process through their delegation.
  4. Prepare supporting documents.

Nominated candidates will be required to submit:

  • A Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • A motivational statement explaining their interest in the programme

For Delegations

Delegations are responsible for forwarding nominated candidates to the OSCE Secretariat through the designated submission channels.

Important Deadline

Delegation Nomination Deadline

17 July 2026 at 23:59 (CEST)

Late nominations may not be considered. Prospective candidates are encouraged to contact their respective delegations well in advance of the deadline to allow sufficient time for the nomination process.

Why This Opportunity Matters

In today’s increasingly interconnected world, effective diplomacy requires a deep understanding of multilateral institutions and international co-operation mechanisms.

The 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme offers a rare opportunity for young diplomats and international affairs professionals to engage directly with experts working at the forefront of security, democracy, human rights, and international co-operation.

By participating in this programme, selected candidates will gain practical knowledge, expand their professional networks, and strengthen their capacity to contribute meaningfully to international diplomacy and co-operative security efforts within their respective countries and regions.

How to Apply

Prospective candidates should contact their relevant OSCE Partner for Co-operation delegation to begin the nomination process.

Click here for more and to apply

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early and ensure all required documents are ready ahead of the nomination deadline of 17 July 2026.

For more global training opportunities click here

 

The post 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme for Young Diplomats: Fully Immersive International Relations Training in Austria and Poland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Catalyst for Change Fellowship 2026–2027 in India: Earn ₹45,000 Monthly While Advancing Human Rights, Migration Justice, and Legal Empowerment
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Migration and Asylum Project (MAP) has officially opened applications for its prestigious Catalyst for Change (CFC) Fellowship 2026–2027, an intensive 24-month fellowship designed to equip emerging professionals with academic, legal, and field experience in migration, women’s rights, protection of displaced communities, and access to justice. Based in Aizawl, Mizoram, this unique fellowship offers participants […]

The post Catalyst for Change Fellowship 2026–2027 in India: Earn ₹45,000 Monthly While Advancing Human Rights, Migration Justice, and Legal Empowerment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Migration and Asylum Project (MAP) has officially opened applications for its prestigious Catalyst for Change (CFC) Fellowship 2026–2027, an intensive 24-month fellowship designed to equip emerging professionals with academic, legal, and field experience in migration, women’s rights, protection of displaced communities, and access to justice.

Based in Aizawl, Mizoram, this unique fellowship offers participants the opportunity to work directly with vulnerable populations while receiving a monthly stipend of ₹45,000 and gaining hands-on experience in legal aid, policy advocacy, research, community engagement, and human rights protection.

For young professionals passionate about social justice, migration governance, legal empowerment, and human rights, the Catalyst for Change Fellowship offers a rare opportunity to contribute meaningfully to some of the most pressing humanitarian and legal challenges of our time.

About the Migration and Asylum Project (MAP)

The Migration and Asylum Project (MAP) is a leading organization working to strengthen legal protection frameworks for migrants, displaced communities, survivors of violence, and other vulnerable populations.

Through legal assistance, advocacy, research, community engagement, and policy interventions, MAP seeks to build a more inclusive and responsive legal protection landscape grounded in the principles of human rights and the rule of law.

The Catalyst for Change Fellowship reflects MAP’s commitment to developing the next generation of human rights practitioners, legal advocates, and social justice leaders.

Fellowship Overview

Position Title

Catalyst for Change (CFC) Fellow

Location

Aizawl, Mizoram, India

Duration

24 Months

Fellowship Start Date

15 August 2026

Monthly Stipend

₹45,000 per month (subject to applicable TDS)

Working Arrangement

On-site

This full-time fellowship combines practical fieldwork, legal engagement, research, advocacy, and professional development.

What Makes This Fellowship Unique?

Unlike many fellowships that focus solely on academic learning, the Catalyst for Change Fellowship integrates practical experience, direct client interaction, legal advocacy, community engagement, and policy work.

Over a two-year period, fellows will develop a deep understanding of migration and displacement issues while working directly with communities affected by these challenges.

The programme is specifically designed to help fellows translate legal and human rights principles into real-world impact.

Areas of Work

Selected fellows may be assigned to one of MAP’s key programmes:

1. Support for Survivors of Sexual and Gender-Based Violence

Fellows may assist in providing legal support and protection services for survivors while contributing to broader efforts aimed at strengthening access to justice and protection mechanisms.

2. Legal Empowerment of Migrant Workers

Participants may work with migrant workers to improve awareness of rights, strengthen access to legal remedies, and address protection concerns affecting vulnerable populations.

3. Direct Assistance to Displaced Communities

Fellows may engage directly with displaced populations, helping them access legal services, protection mechanisms, and justice systems.

These assignments provide fellows with practical exposure to complex humanitarian and legal issues affecting vulnerable communities.

What Fellows Will Gain

The Catalyst for Change Fellowship is structured to provide a comprehensive learning experience.

Academic and Legal Expertise

Participants will gain:

  • Advanced understanding of migration law
  • Knowledge of international human rights frameworks
  • Exposure to refugee and displacement issues
  • Experience applying legal principles to real-world cases
  • Understanding of access-to-justice mechanisms

Field Experience

Fellows will work directly with affected communities and gain:

  • Community engagement experience
  • Case management skills
  • Client interaction experience
  • Exposure to humanitarian contexts
  • Practical understanding of protection challenges

Policy and Advocacy Skills

Participants will contribute to:

  • Policy research
  • Advocacy initiatives
  • Legal reform efforts
  • Rights awareness campaigns
  • Strategic interventions supporting vulnerable populations

Research Opportunities

Fellows will participate in:

  • Evidence-based research
  • Human rights documentation
  • Policy analysis
  • Programme evaluations
  • Publications and reports

Professional Networking

The programme provides opportunities to engage with:

  • United Nations agencies
  • Civil society organizations
  • Human rights practitioners
  • Government institutions
  • Legal experts
  • International migration specialists

Core Responsibilities

Depending on programme placement, fellows may undertake several responsibilities.

Legal Aid and Representation

Fellows may:

  • Provide legal assistance to migrants and displaced persons
  • Support case preparation
  • Facilitate access to legal remedies
  • Assist clients navigating justice systems

Community Outreach and Awareness

Responsibilities may include:

  • Conducting legal awareness sessions
  • Organizing community meetings
  • Facilitating rights education programmes
  • Promoting access to justice initiatives

Community Legal Desks

Fellows may help establish and operationalize community legal desks to:

  • Receive client intakes
  • Identify legal needs
  • Provide referrals
  • Deliver individualized assistance

Client Interviews and Protection Assessments

Participants may:

  • Conduct trauma-informed interviews
  • Identify protection concerns
  • Prepare supporting legal documentation
  • Support vulnerable individuals throughout legal processes

Research, Policy, and Advocacy

Fellows will contribute to:

  • Programme-based research
  • Policy analysis
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Legal reform initiatives
  • Human rights documentation

Community Network Strengthening

The fellowship includes supporting grassroots networks comprising:

  • Paralegal volunteers
  • Community leaders
  • Local organizations
  • Civil society actors

Administrative and Programme Support

Additional duties may include:

  • Database management
  • Documentation
  • Correspondence drafting
  • Programme coordination activities

Training and Mentorship

Throughout the fellowship, participants will receive supervision and mentorship from experienced project coordinators and practitioners.

This guidance helps fellows develop both technical competencies and professional skills necessary for long-term careers in the development and human rights sectors.

Career Opportunities After the Fellowship

The Catalyst for Change Fellowship has proven to be an excellent launchpad for future opportunities.

Former fellows have successfully advanced to careers in:

  • United Nations agencies
  • International NGOs
  • Human rights organizations
  • Development institutions
  • Research organizations
  • Government agencies
  • Academic institutions

Many alumni have also pursued:

  • Graduate degrees
  • Doctoral studies
  • Prestigious academic fellowships
  • International research opportunities

Who Should Apply?

MAP seeks highly motivated individuals who demonstrate:

  • Passion for social justice
  • Commitment to human rights
  • Interest in migration and displacement issues
  • Dedication to improving access to justice
  • Desire to work directly with vulnerable populations

Candidates from law and related disciplines are encouraged to apply.

Residents of Mizoram and fluent Mizo speakers are particularly encouraged to submit applications.

Diversity and Inclusion

MAP is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering diversity and inclusion.

Applications are particularly encouraged from:

  • Women
  • Individuals from marginalized communities
  • Gender minorities
  • Underrepresented groups

The organization values diverse perspectives and seeks to build an inclusive workforce committed to social justice.

Recruitment Process

MAP’s recruitment process generally takes approximately six to eight weeks.

Due to the large number of applications typically received, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Applicants who are shortlisted will receive further communication regarding subsequent recruitment stages.

Any changes to the recruitment timeline will be communicated directly to shortlisted candidates.

How to Apply

Interested candidates must complete the official application form provided by MAP.

Applicants should carefully review all requirements and ensure that all requested information is submitted accurately.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

30 June 2026 at 5:00 PM (IST).

Candidates are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid any last-minute issues.

Contact Information

For questions regarding the fellowship, applicants may contact:

Email: recruitments@aratrust.in

Subject Line: Application for the post of M.A.P. Catalyst for Change Fellowship 2026–2027

Final Thoughts

The Catalyst for Change Fellowship 2026–2027 offers a transformative opportunity for emerging professionals interested in human rights, migration, displacement, legal empowerment, and social justice.

With a competitive monthly stipend, extensive field exposure, mentorship from experts, opportunities to engage with international organizations, and the chance to directly impact the lives of vulnerable communities, this fellowship stands out as one of the most meaningful early-career opportunities in the human rights and legal development sectors.

For aspiring changemakers committed to advancing justice, dignity, and protection for marginalized communities, the Catalyst for Change Fellowship may be the ideal next step toward a rewarding and impactful career.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Catalyst for Change Fellowship 2026–2027 in India: Earn ₹45,000 Monthly While Advancing Human Rights, Migration Justice, and Legal Empowerment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women SIARP 2.0 Call for Proposals 2026: African Civil Society Organizations Can Access Up to USD 548,000 to Advance Gender Equality, End Violence Against Women and Girls, and Strengthen Women’s Rights Movements Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women) has launched a major Call for Proposals (CFP) under the Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme (SIARP) 2.0, inviting eligible civil society organizations (CSOs), women’s rights organizations, youth-led organizations, coalitions, consortiums, networks, research institutions, and other non-profit entities operating across Africa to […]

The post UN Women SIARP 2.0 Call for Proposals 2026: African Civil Society Organizations Can Access Up to USD 548,000 to Advance Gender Equality, End Violence Against Women and Girls, and Strengthen Women’s Rights Movements Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women) has launched a major Call for Proposals (CFP) under the Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme (SIARP) 2.0, inviting eligible civil society organizations (CSOs), women’s rights organizations, youth-led organizations, coalitions, consortiums, networks, research institutions, and other non-profit entities operating across Africa to apply for substantial grant funding.

This highly competitive funding opportunity aims to strengthen efforts to eliminate violence against women and girls (EVAWG), combat harmful practices, promote sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR), strengthen survivor-centred services, support women’s rights and youth-led movements, and accelerate policy reforms and accountability mechanisms across the African continent.

With funding ranging from USD 110,000 to USD 548,000 depending on the selected component, SIARP 2.0 represents one of the most significant regional investments currently available for organizations working to advance gender equality, women’s empowerment, and social justice across Africa.

Organizations may apply individually or through partnerships, coalitions, and consortiums to maximize impact, strengthen implementation capacity, and expand regional reach.

What is the Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme (SIARP) 2.0?

The Spotlight Initiative is the United Nations’ flagship global initiative dedicated to ending violence against women and girls through coordinated, survivor-centred, and rights-based approaches.

SIARP 2.0 builds upon the achievements and lessons learned from the first phase of the programme (2020–2023), which supported advocacy, policy reforms, institutional strengthening, and civil society engagement across Africa.

The programme is implemented jointly by:

  • UN Women
  • United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
  • United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA)
  • United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF)

Working alongside:

  • African Union Commission (AUC)
  • European Union (EU)
  • Regional Economic Communities (RECs)
  • African Union Member States
  • Women’s Rights Organizations
  • Youth-led organizations
  • Civil society networks
  • Survivor-led groups
  • Faith-based institutions
  • Traditional leadership structures
  • Disability rights organizations

The programme adopts a comprehensive human-rights based approach designed to address structural inequalities and the root causes of violence against women and girls while advancing sustainable and long-term systems change.

Why SIARP 2.0 Matters for Africa

Despite significant progress across Africa, millions of women and girls continue to experience:

  • Gender-based violence
  • Child marriage
  • Female genital mutilation (FGM)
  • Sexual violence
  • Economic exclusion
  • Discriminatory social norms
  • Barriers to accessing justice
  • Limited access to sexual and reproductive health services

The programme recognizes that ending violence against women and girls requires more than isolated projects. It requires coordinated action among governments, civil society organizations, youth groups, regional institutions, survivors, traditional leaders, religious leaders, and development partners.

SIARP 2.0 therefore focuses on transforming systems, policies, institutions, social norms, and accountability mechanisms across Africa.

Funding Opportunity Overview

UN Women intends to select between three and seven Responsible Parties (RPs) to implement specific programme components under SIARP 2.0.

Applicants may submit proposals covering:

  • A minimum of one component
  • A maximum of three components

Multiple organizations may be selected under the same component area to ensure:

  • Geographic representation
  • Diverse expertise
  • Strong implementation capacity
  • Regional and continental coverage

Organizations are strongly encouraged to form:

  • Consortiums
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Regional alliances
  • Cross-country collaborations

to strengthen their proposals and increase implementation effectiveness.

Funding Available Under SIARP 2.0

Organizations may access substantial funding depending on the component selected.

Component 1

Maputo Protocol Advocacy and Participation of Women Human Rights Defenders

Maximum Funding:
USD 315,000

Component 2

Civil Society Mobilization for Member State Investment and Resource Mobilization

Maximum Funding:
USD 110,000

Component 3

Countering Pushback Against Gender Equality and Mobilizing Faith-Based and Traditional Leaders

Maximum Funding:
USD 300,000

Component 4

Women’s Economic Empowerment and Violence-Free Workplaces

Maximum Funding:
USD 150,000

Component 5

Survivor-Centred Standards and Accountability for Essential Services

Maximum Funding:
USD 200,000

Component 6

Strengthening Regional Women’s Rights Platforms and Civil Society Resilience

Maximum Funding:
USD 548,000

Component 7

Partnerships with Organizations of Persons with Disabilities and Marginalized Groups

Maximum Funding:
USD 200,000

Funding requests exceeding component ceilings may not be considered during evaluation.

SIARP 2.0 Strategic Outcome Areas

The programme is built around four interconnected outcomes designed to create lasting impact across Africa.

Outcome A: Laws, Policies, Institutions and Data Systems

This outcome focuses on strengthening:

  • Legal frameworks
  • National policies
  • Institutional accountability
  • Gender-responsive governance systems
  • Evidence generation
  • Data collection mechanisms

The goal is to ensure that laws and policies addressing violence against women and girls are effectively implemented and enforced.

Outcome B: Prevention and Social Norms Transformation

This outcome seeks to address the root causes of violence by:

  • Challenging harmful social norms
  • Addressing backlash against gender equality
  • Promoting positive masculinity
  • Strengthening prevention initiatives
  • Supporting SRHR advocacy

Outcome C: Access to Essential Services

This outcome focuses on improving access to:

  • Survivor-centred support services
  • Legal aid
  • Healthcare services
  • Psychosocial support
  • Shelters
  • Safe spaces
  • One-stop centres
  • Justice systems

Outcome D: Strengthening Women’s Rights and Youth-Led Movements

This outcome seeks to strengthen:

  • Women’s rights organizations
  • Youth-led movements
  • Civil society networks
  • Civic space
  • Advocacy platforms
  • Accountability mechanisms

Detailed Breakdown of the Seven Funding Components

Component 1: Maputo Protocol Advocacy and Women Human Rights Defenders

This component focuses on advancing implementation of the Maputo Protocol and strengthening participation of women human rights defenders in continental and regional accountability processes.

Key activities include:

  • Advocacy for universal ratification of the Maputo Protocol
  • Support for domestication and implementation efforts
  • Strengthening participation of Women Human Rights Defenders (WHRDs)
  • Enhancing engagement in reporting and accountability mechanisms

Funding Available:
USD 315,000

Component 2: Resource Mobilization and Investment Advocacy

Organizations will support efforts aimed at increasing public investment and financing for:

  • Gender equality
  • Ending violence against women and girls
  • Harmful practices
  • Sexual and reproductive health and rights

Funding Available:
USD 110,000

Component 3: Social Norms Transformation and Countering Backlash

This component addresses growing resistance to gender equality and SRHR across parts of Africa.

Organizations will:

  • Develop advocacy campaigns
  • Engage religious leaders
  • Engage traditional leaders
  • Strengthen regional coalitions
  • Promote positive social norms

Funding Available:
USD 300,000

Component 4: Women’s Economic Empowerment

Organizations will support:

  • Women entrepreneurs
  • Women workers
  • Trade unions
  • Cross-border traders
  • Youth-led networks

The objective is to create safer and more inclusive economic opportunities for women and girls.

Funding Available:
USD 150,000

Component 5: Survivor-Centred Essential Services

This component prioritizes:

  • Quality standards for services
  • Survivor participation
  • Accountability mechanisms
  • Shelter strengthening
  • Safe spaces
  • One-stop centres

Funding Available:
USD 200,000

Component 6: Strengthening Women’s Rights and Youth-Led Organizations

As the largest funding component, this area seeks to:

  • Strengthen organizational resilience
  • Build institutional capacity
  • Support learning exchanges
  • Create advocacy platforms
  • Establish the SIARP 2.0 Civil Society Regional Reference Group

Funding Available:
USD 548,000

Component 7: Disability Inclusion and Marginalized Communities

Organizations working with persons with disabilities and marginalized communities will be supported to:

  • Build partnerships with AU institutions
  • Engage Regional Economic Communities
  • Advance gender equality commitments
  • Promote inclusion in policy processes

Funding Available:
USD 200,000

Geographic Scope

The programme covers all African Union Member States.

Priority is given to organizations operating at:

  • Continental level
  • Regional level
  • Sub-regional level
  • Multi-country level

Organizations with strong national experience may also apply if they can demonstrate regional partnerships and broader influence.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants include:

  • Civil Society Organizations
  • Women’s Rights Organizations
  • Youth-led Organizations
  • Coalitions
  • Consortiums
  • Networks
  • Research Institutions
  • Survivor-led Organizations
  • Disability Rights Organizations
  • Non-profit Organizations

Applicants must demonstrate significant expertise and experience in areas relevant to the selected programme components.

Organizational Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be legally registered
  • Have at least five years of operational experience
  • Operate within Africa
  • Demonstrate strong governance structures
  • Show financial management capacity
  • Have safeguarding mechanisms
  • Maintain anti-fraud systems
  • Implement PSEA policies and procedures

Duration of the Programme

The anticipated implementation period is:

Start Date:
1 October 2026

End Date:
December 2028

Total Duration:
27 Months

Organizations must submit detailed workplans covering the entire implementation period.

Application Process

Organizations must prepare comprehensive technical and financial proposals and submit them electronically.

Required documents include:

  • Proposal submission template
  • Technical proposal
  • Financial proposal
  • Organizational registration documents
  • Governance documents
  • Audited financial statements
  • Personnel CVs
  • Procurement policies
  • Anti-fraud policies
  • Capacity assessment documents

Call for Proposals Template for Responsible Parties – SIARP 2.0 _ENGLISH

Call for Proposals Template for Responsible Parties- SIARP 2.0 _FRENCH

All proposals must be sent to spotlight.stream1@unwomen.org

Proposal Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be evaluated in two phases.

Phase I: Technical Evaluation (70 Points)

Areas assessed include:

  • Compliance with requirements
  • Organizational relevance
  • Technical capacity
  • Understanding of the Terms of Reference
  • Implementation strategy

Applicants must score at least 50 points to advance.

Phase II: Financial Evaluation (30 Points)

Financial proposals will be scored based on competitiveness and cost-effectiveness.

Important Dates

Requests for Clarification:
22 June – 8 July 2026

Virtual Pre-Proposal Conferences:
24 June 2026
7 July 2026

Proposal Submission Deadline:
27 July 2026 at 5:00 PM East Africa Time

Award Notification:
18 September 2026

Project Start Date:
1 October 2026

Project Completion:
December 2028

Contact Information

For questions and proposal submissions:

Contact Person:
Ibrahim Nurudeen

Email:
spotlight.stream1@unwomen.org

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the UN Women SIARP 2.0 Call for Proposals 2026.

Conclusion

The UN Women Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme (SIARP) 2.0 presents a transformative opportunity for African civil society organizations to secure significant funding while contributing to one of the continent’s most ambitious initiatives focused on ending violence against women and girls, advancing gender equality, promoting sexual and reproductive health and rights, and strengthening women’s rights and youth-led movements.

With grants of up to USD 548,000, continental reach, strong institutional partnerships, and a 27-month implementation period, SIARP 2.0 offers organizations the resources needed to influence policy, strengthen institutions, empower communities, transform harmful social norms, and create lasting change for women and girls across Africa.

Organizations with strong regional experience, innovative approaches, and a commitment to advancing gender justice are encouraged to prepare competitive proposals and seize this important funding opportunity before the application deadline.

Discover more global Grant, African, and Women opportunities on OFY.

The post UN Women SIARP 2.0 Call for Proposals 2026: African Civil Society Organizations Can Access Up to USD 548,000 to Advance Gender Equality, End Violence Against Women and Girls, and Strengthen Women’s Rights Movements Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OSCE Youth Program 2026 in Poland: Fully Funded Human Rights and Civic Engagement Fellowship for Muslim Youth Across the OSCE Region
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Young leaders play a critical role in building inclusive, resilient, and tolerant societies. Recognizing the importance of youth participation in addressing discrimination and promoting human rights, the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) has launched an exciting call for applications for its Human Rights-Based Civic Engagement and Alliance Building Program: Supporting Muslim Youth […]

The post OSCE Youth Program 2026 in Poland: Fully Funded Human Rights and Civic Engagement Fellowship for Muslim Youth Across the OSCE Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders play a critical role in building inclusive, resilient, and tolerant societies. Recognizing the importance of youth participation in addressing discrimination and promoting human rights, the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) has launched an exciting call for applications for its Human Rights-Based Civic Engagement and Alliance Building Program: Supporting Muslim Youth Participation at the Local Level.

This fully funded opportunity will bring together Muslim youth from across the OSCE region for an intensive learning, networking, and action-oriented experience designed to strengthen civic engagement, promote human rights, and empower participants to address intolerance and discrimination against Muslims within their communities.

The program combines expert-led training, practical project implementation, mentorship, peer collaboration, and contribution to a regional knowledge resource that will support future youth leaders and activists.

Applications are now open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the deadline of 30 June 2026.

About the OSCE Youth Program

The Human Rights-Based Civic Engagement and Alliance Building Program is an initiative aimed at empowering Muslim youth to become stronger advocates for human rights, social inclusion, and community engagement.

Through a combination of training, practical action, mentorship, and collaborative learning, participants will gain the skills, knowledge, and tools necessary to address challenges facing Muslim communities and contribute positively to social cohesion at the local level.

The program emphasizes human rights-based approaches to civic participation and focuses on building alliances that can help tackle discrimination, intolerance, exclusion, and hate speech.

Participants will engage with experts, mentors, and fellow youth leaders from different countries, creating a valuable network of peers committed to fostering inclusive communities.

Program Timeline

The fellowship consists of three interconnected phases that provide participants with both theoretical knowledge and practical experience.

Phase 1: Training Workshop in Warsaw, Poland

Dates: 25–27 August 2026

The program begins with a three-day intensive workshop in Warsaw, Poland.

During this training, participants will learn from leading experts in:

  • Human rights
  • Civic engagement
  • Social inclusion
  • Community advocacy
  • Combating intolerance and discrimination against Muslims
  • Youth leadership
  • Alliance building
  • Community mobilization

The workshop will provide a collaborative environment where participants can exchange ideas, discuss local challenges, and develop innovative approaches to addressing discrimination within their communities.

Phase 2: Community-Based Project Implementation

Period: September – November 2026

Following the workshop, participants will design and implement a project through their affiliated organization or community group.

The projects should focus on addressing specific forms of intolerance and discrimination against Muslims at the local level.

Participants will be encouraged to apply the knowledge and skills gained during the Warsaw training to create meaningful community impact.

Potential project areas may include:

  • Human rights education
  • Community awareness campaigns
  • Youth engagement initiatives
  • Social inclusion activities
  • Intercultural dialogue
  • Countering misinformation
  • Digital advocacy campaigns
  • Anti-discrimination awareness programs
  • Community dialogue platforms

This practical phase ensures that learning is transformed into tangible action and measurable community outcomes.

Phase 3: Mentorship and Final Report Development

Period: September – December 2026

Throughout the project implementation phase, participants will receive ongoing support from experienced mentors through follow-up Zoom sessions.

The mentorship component aims to:

  • Guide project implementation
  • Support problem-solving
  • Strengthen leadership capacity
  • Enhance project impact
  • Facilitate peer learning

At the conclusion of the program, participants will contribute to a joint report documenting lessons learned, best practices, challenges, and recommendations.

The report will serve as a valuable resource for future youth activists and organizations interested in implementing similar initiatives.

The final report is expected to be completed in December 2026.

What Participants Will Learn

Participants will gain comprehensive knowledge and practical competencies in several critical areas.

1. Human Rights-Based Education

Participants will develop a deeper understanding of:

  • International human rights standards
  • Equality and non-discrimination principles
  • Human rights advocacy
  • Rights-based community engagement approaches

2. Civic Engagement and Advocacy

The program will equip participants with tools to:

  • Engage local communities effectively
  • Influence positive social change
  • Build advocacy campaigns
  • Mobilize stakeholders
  • Strengthen youth participation

3. Digital Literacy and Countering Online Hate Speech

As online platforms increasingly shape public discourse, participants will learn strategies for:

  • Identifying online hate speech
  • Countering harmful narratives
  • Promoting positive digital engagement
  • Enhancing digital resilience
  • Using digital tools for advocacy

4. Alliance Building and Collaboration

Participants will gain skills in:

  • Building partnerships
  • Coalition development
  • Cross-sector collaboration
  • Community networking
  • Strategic relationship building

5. Leadership and Project Management

The fellowship will strengthen participants’ abilities to:

  • Lead community initiatives
  • Design projects
  • Manage implementation processes
  • Monitor progress
  • Evaluate project outcomes

Benefits of Participating

Selected participants will enjoy numerous benefits throughout the fellowship.

Fully Funded International Training

Participants will attend a three-day workshop in Warsaw, Poland, led by experts in human rights and anti-discrimination efforts.

Practical Community Impact

Participants will have the opportunity to design and implement projects that directly address challenges within their communities.

Professional Mentorship

Experienced mentors will provide ongoing guidance and support throughout the project cycle.

International Networking

The fellowship will bring together Muslim youth leaders from diverse countries and backgrounds, creating opportunities for long-term collaboration and peer learning.

Enhanced Leadership Skills

Participants will develop practical competencies in advocacy, civic engagement, project management, and human rights promotion.

Contribution to Regional Knowledge

Participants will contribute to a collective report that may influence future initiatives supporting youth participation and inclusion.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Across many societies, Muslim communities continue to face challenges related to discrimination, exclusion, stereotypes, and intolerance.

Young people are often among the most effective agents of change in addressing these issues.

By empowering Muslim youth with knowledge, networks, resources, and practical experience, the OSCE aims to strengthen community resilience and promote more inclusive societies throughout the region.

This fellowship represents an opportunity not only for personal and professional growth but also for meaningful social impact.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 30 June 2026 (23:59 CEST / Vienna Time)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline, as incomplete applications will not be considered.

Acceptance Notification

Successful applicants will be notified on:

17 July 2026

Important Application Note

Applicants should ensure that all required information is submitted accurately and completely.

Incomplete applications will not be reviewed or considered during the selection process.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official application portal:

 

Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their application materials early and carefully demonstrate their commitment to human rights, civic engagement, and community leadership.

Final Thoughts

The OSCE Youth Program 2026 offers a unique platform for Muslim youth leaders across the OSCE region to strengthen their skills, expand their networks, and create meaningful change within their communities.

Through expert training in Warsaw, practical community-based projects, ongoing mentorship, and international collaboration, participants will gain valuable tools to address intolerance and discrimination while advancing human rights and civic engagement.

For young people passionate about inclusion, social justice, youth leadership, and community development, this fully funded fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity to learn, lead, and make a lasting impact.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post OSCE Youth Program 2026 in Poland: Fully Funded Human Rights and Civic Engagement Fellowship for Muslim Youth Across the OSCE Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CNN London Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple Journalism, Sports, Video Editing, and Newsroom Opportunities in the UK
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The global media industry continues to offer exceptional opportunities for aspiring journalists and media professionals, and CNN has once again opened several exciting internship and apprenticeship positions in London, United Kingdom. These opportunities provide aspiring media professionals with direct exposure to one of the world’s most influential news organizations. For students, recent graduates, and early-career […]

The post CNN London Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple Journalism, Sports, Video Editing, and Newsroom Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global media industry continues to offer exceptional opportunities for aspiring journalists and media professionals, and CNN has once again opened several exciting internship and apprenticeship positions in London, United Kingdom. These opportunities provide aspiring media professionals with direct exposure to one of the world’s most influential news organizations.

For students, recent graduates, and early-career professionals looking to launch a career in journalism, broadcasting, digital media, sports reporting, content creation, video production, or newsroom operations, CNN’s 2026 London internship opportunities represent an invaluable stepping stone toward professional success.

Through hands-on newsroom experience, mentorship from industry experts, and exposure to international news coverage, successful applicants will gain practical skills that are highly sought after across the global media landscape.

Why CNN Internships Matter

CNN is recognized worldwide for delivering breaking news, in-depth reporting, investigative journalism, and international coverage across television, digital platforms, and social media.

Interning at CNN allows participants to:

  • Work alongside experienced journalists and producers.
  • Gain exposure to international news operations.
  • Develop professional reporting and storytelling skills.
  • Learn newsroom workflows and editorial processes.
  • Build a professional network within the media industry.
  • Gain practical experience that strengthens future job applications.
  • Understand multimedia journalism across TV, digital, and social platforms.

These internships provide a rare opportunity to learn directly from professionals operating at the highest levels of global journalism.

Available CNN London Internship and Apprenticeship Opportunities

CNN is currently offering the following early-career opportunities in London:

1. CNN London Sports Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

CNN International’s flagship sports program, World Sport, airs multiple times daily throughout the week.

The Sports Intern will contribute to:

  • Sports news coverage
  • Research activities
  • High-profile athlete interviews
  • Story development
  • Production support
  • Coverage of major sporting events
  • Exploration of important themes such as racism, diversity, inclusion, and gender issues in sports

This role is ideal for candidates passionate about sports journalism and international sports coverage.

2. CNN News Desk Reporting Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

CNN’s award-winning London News Desk is seeking aspiring journalists interested in international and breaking news.

Responsibilities may include:

  • News gathering
  • Research and verification
  • Reporting assistance
  • Monitoring global news developments
  • Supporting newsroom operations
  • Assisting journalists covering breaking stories

This internship offers exposure to real-time news production and international reporting.

3. CNNIC Content Sales & Strategic Partnerships Apprentice

Location: London, United Kingdom

This apprenticeship combines business development, content strategy, and client engagement.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Researching consumer and client needs
  • Supporting content sales initiatives
  • Building client relationships
  • Identifying new partnership opportunities
  • Working with internal teams
  • Supporting strategic growth initiatives

This role is suitable for candidates interested in media business, marketing, partnerships, and content commercialization.

4. CNN London Planning (Interviews & Guest Booking) Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

The Planning Desk sits at the center of CNN’s newsroom operations.

Responsibilities include:

  • Identifying compelling interview opportunities
  • Booking guests for CNN programs
  • Supporting breaking news coverage
  • Researching experts and commentators
  • Coordinating interviews
  • Assisting editorial planning teams

Candidates interested in newsroom coordination and editorial planning will find this role particularly valuable.

5. CNN London Features Video Editor Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

CNN International Productions develops feature content for television, digital platforms, and social media.

Interns will:

  • Assist editors with production workflows
  • Prepare video edits
  • Create feature content
  • Support television and digital productions
  • Work on short-form storytelling projects
  • Learn professional editing techniques

This opportunity is excellent for aspiring video editors and multimedia storytellers.

6. CNN London News Desk Video Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

The Video Newsdesk plays a central role in CNN’s global storytelling operations.

Key duties include:

  • Producing video content
  • Supporting digital and broadcast teams
  • Managing multiple projects simultaneously
  • Creating high-quality visual news content
  • Assisting breaking news coverage
  • Supporting newsroom video operations

Candidates with strong multimedia and video production interests are encouraged to apply.

7. CNN London Features (TV & Digital) Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

This internship supports CNN International Productions across television and digital content.

Intern responsibilities may include:

  • Story research
  • Editorial planning
  • Script writing
  • Production assistance
  • Content development
  • Supporting television and digital storytelling initiatives

The role provides valuable experience in long-form feature production and multimedia journalism.

Benefits of Joining CNN’s Internship Program

Successful interns may benefit from:

Professional Development

  • Real-world newsroom experience
  • Exposure to international journalism standards
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals
  • Skill development in reporting, production, and storytelling

Career Advancement

  • Enhanced CV and portfolio
  • Networking opportunities
  • Industry exposure
  • Improved employability within global media organizations

Practical Experience

  • Participation in live newsroom environments
  • Exposure to breaking news operations
  • Multimedia production experience
  • Hands-on project involvement

Skills Candidates Can Develop

Participants can strengthen competencies in:

  • Journalism
  • News reporting
  • Broadcast production
  • Video editing
  • Script writing
  • Story development
  • Research and verification
  • Interview coordination
  • Digital storytelling
  • Social media content production
  • Strategic communication
  • Partnership management

How to Apply

Interested candidates should:

  1. Visit the official Warner Bros. Discovery Early Careers portal.
  2. Search for the desired internship or apprenticeship position.
  3. Review eligibility requirements carefully.
  4. Prepare an updated CV and supporting documents.
  5. Submit the application before positions are filled.

Because internship opportunities at major international media organizations attract significant competition, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Application Deadline

No specific application deadline has been announced.

Applications are likely to remain open until positions are filled. Early submission is strongly recommended.

Final Thoughts

CNN’s 2026 London internship opportunities provide a unique pathway into the world of international journalism, sports media, television production, digital storytelling, and newsroom operations. Whether your ambition is to become a reporter, producer, editor, sports journalist, content strategist, or media executive, these internships offer hands-on experience within one of the world’s most respected news organizations.

For aspiring media professionals seeking meaningful exposure to global journalism and broadcasting, these opportunities could be the ideal launchpad for a successful career.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post CNN London Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple Journalism, Sports, Video Editing, and Newsroom Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers Launch Grant Opportunity to Support Young Women’s Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Organizations working to improve youth employment, digital inclusion, and women’s economic empowerment now have an exciting opportunity to partner with UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers through a new Request for Proposal (RFP) published on the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM). The funding opportunity, titled “Request for Proposal for Links to Work Programme — Supporting […]

The post UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers Launch Grant Opportunity to Support Young Women’s Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to improve youth employment, digital inclusion, and women’s economic empowerment now have an exciting opportunity to partner with UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers through a new Request for Proposal (RFP) published on the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM).

The funding opportunity, titled “Request for Proposal for Links to Work Programme — Supporting Young Women Aged 18–24 from Underserved Backgrounds to Access Sustainable Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem in Partnership with UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers,” seeks qualified organizations to implement initiatives that help young women build employability skills and transition into sustainable employment opportunities.

This initiative reflects a shared commitment to expanding access to decent work, digital opportunities, and economic empowerment for underserved young women in Kenya through innovative partnerships and technology-driven solutions.

About the Opportunity

The Links to Work Programme is designed to strengthen employment pathways for young women by leveraging the Yoma digital ecosystem, an innovative platform that connects young people with learning opportunities, skills development, work experiences, and employment opportunities.

Through this Request for Proposal, UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers are seeking capable implementation partners that can support young women in overcoming barriers to employment while strengthening their digital and professional skills.

The programme specifically targets young women aged 18 to 24 years from underserved communities, recognizing that many continue to face significant social, economic, and structural challenges in accessing quality employment opportunities.

Programme Objectives

The selected implementing organization(s) will contribute to advancing youth employment by supporting activities that help participants prepare for and secure sustainable employment.

The programme aims to:

  • Improve employability among underserved young women.
  • Expand access to digital opportunities through the Yoma platform.
  • Strengthen workforce readiness.
  • Promote sustainable employment outcomes.
  • Support women’s economic empowerment.
  • Increase access to skills development opportunities.
  • Foster partnerships that connect young women to employers and employment ecosystems.

Focus on the Yoma Digital Ecosystem

The initiative places significant emphasis on the Yoma digital ecosystem, which serves as a platform connecting young people with opportunities that improve their employment prospects.

Through Yoma, participants may benefit from opportunities such as:

  • Digital learning.
  • Skills development.
  • Career readiness activities.
  • Employment pathways.
  • Youth engagement opportunities.
  • Connections to employers and ecosystem partners.

By integrating participants into this digital ecosystem, the programme seeks to create long-term employment outcomes rather than short-term interventions.

Who Should Apply?

Organizations with strong experience in youth employment, workforce development, digital skills training, and women’s empowerment are encouraged to submit proposals.

Potential applicants include:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Civil society organizations
  • Youth development organizations
  • Women’s empowerment organizations
  • Workforce development agencies
  • Registered training institutions
  • Social enterprises
  • Private sector organizations
  • Partnerships and consortia

Organizations should possess the technical expertise and operational capacity required to implement high-quality employment programmes targeting underserved young women.

Expected Areas of Expertise

Successful applicants should demonstrate experience in areas such as:

  • Youth employment programming
  • Employability training
  • Career development
  • Digital skills training
  • Entrepreneurship support
  • Women’s economic empowerment
  • Community outreach
  • Workforce development
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Partnership management

Benefits of Participating

Organizations selected through this Request for Proposal may benefit from:

  • Partnership with UNICEF Kenya.
  • Collaboration with Standard Chartered Futuremakers.
  • Opportunity to contribute to national youth employment goals.
  • Increased organizational visibility.
  • Strengthened institutional partnerships.
  • Opportunity to improve employment outcomes for underserved young women.
  • Participation in an innovative digital employment ecosystem.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Youth unemployment remains one of the most pressing development challenges affecting many countries, including Kenya. Young women often experience additional barriers due to limited access to education, technology, employment networks, and financial resources.

By investing in innovative employment pathways through the Yoma digital ecosystem, this programme seeks to close opportunity gaps while supporting inclusive economic growth and sustainable livelihoods.

The initiative also aligns with broader efforts to advance gender equality, digital inclusion, and decent work for young people.

Application Process

Interested organizations should carefully review the complete Request for Proposal published on the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM).

Applicants should ensure that they understand all submission requirements, eligibility criteria, technical specifications, and proposal guidelines before preparing their applications.

Organizations are encouraged to prepare strong technical proposals that clearly demonstrate:

  • Organizational capacity.
  • Relevant implementation experience.
  • Technical expertise.
  • Understanding of youth employment challenges.
  • Ability to deliver measurable employment outcomes.
  • Strong monitoring and reporting systems.

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been provided in the available announcement.

Interested organizations should visit the official UNGM procurement opportunity page and submit their proposals as early as possible once all required documentation has been prepared.

How to Apply

Applications should be submitted through the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM) procurement platform.

Applicants should access the official procurement notice, review all proposal requirements, and follow the submission instructions provided in the Request for Proposal documentation before submitting their applications.

Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers Launch Grant Opportunity to Support Young Women’s Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Policy & Advocacy Fellow at Anthropocene Alliance (A2): Fully Remote U.S. Environmental Justice Job Paying $65,000 Per Year
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Anthropocene Alliance (A2), the largest coalition of frontline communities fighting for environmental justice across the United States and its territories, is currently accepting applications for a full-time Policy & Advocacy Fellow. This exciting fully remote opportunity is designed for professionals who are passionate about public policy, grassroots advocacy, climate justice, and community empowerment. Offering […]

The post Policy & Advocacy Fellow at Anthropocene Alliance (A2): Fully Remote U.S. Environmental Justice Job Paying $65,000 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Anthropocene Alliance (A2), the largest coalition of frontline communities fighting for environmental justice across the United States and its territories, is currently accepting applications for a full-time Policy & Advocacy Fellow. This exciting fully remote opportunity is designed for professionals who are passionate about public policy, grassroots advocacy, climate justice, and community empowerment.

Offering an annual salary of $65,000 plus benefits, this position provides an exceptional opportunity to work alongside one of the nation’s leading environmental justice organizations while helping frontline communities transform local environmental challenges into coordinated policy action.

If you have experience in public policy, advocacy, community organizing, or environmental justice and are looking to make a meaningful impact at a national level, this could be the ideal opportunity.

About Anthropocene Alliance (A2)

Anthropocene Alliance (A2) is the largest coalition of frontline communities in the United States dedicated to environmental protection and environmental justice.

The organization currently represents more than 430 member organizations working across the United States and its territories. These grassroots groups are directly affected by climate change, pollution, flooding, mining, environmental degradation, and other environmental challenges.

Rather than working independently, A2 helps unite these organizations, strengthens their collective voice, and supports coordinated advocacy efforts aimed at influencing environmental policies and promoting long-term systems change.

About the Policy & Advocacy Fellow Position

The Policy & Advocacy Fellow will play a central role in strengthening the policy capacity of frontline communities.

Working closely with member organizations, the successful candidate will help transform local environmental concerns into coordinated advocacy campaigns that can influence policy at state and local levels.

This is a full-time position (40 hours per week) and is completely remote, allowing qualified applicants based anywhere in the United States to apply.

The appointment is initially for two years, with the possibility of becoming a permanent position depending on future funding.

The role is funded through the newly established Strengthening Human Infrastructure Project (SHIP) at the Aspen Global Change Institute, where Anthropocene Alliance serves as one of the core partners.

Salary and Benefits

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Annual salary of $65,000
  • Comprehensive employee benefits
  • Fully remote work arrangement
  • Opportunity to collaborate with national environmental justice leaders
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Experience working within a major national coalition

Key Responsibilities

The Policy & Advocacy Fellow will contribute across three major areas:

1. Campaign Strategy and Networked Action

The successful candidate will:

  • Analyze advocacy campaigns led by A2 member organizations.
  • Identify opportunities for coordinated action across multiple states.
  • Develop practical campaign strategies that can be adapted by organizations with varying levels of capacity.
  • Produce advocacy resources including:
    • Model ordinances
    • Sample legislation
    • Campaign planning guides
    • Messaging frameworks
    • Advocacy toolkits
    • Talking points
  • Coordinate multi-state advocacy campaigns.
  • Organize national and regional “Days of Action.”
  • Track campaign outcomes and policy achievements.

2. Member Training and Capacity Building

Responsibilities include:

  • Leading policy and advocacy trainings.
  • Coaching grassroots organizations.
  • Building members’ understanding of policy processes.
  • Supporting organizations engaging with:
    • Public Service Commissions
    • Zoning boards
    • Local government agencies
    • Regional authorities
  • Helping members prepare:
    • Public comments
    • Testimony
    • Advocacy actions

It is important to note that this role does not involve direct lobbying activities.

3. Learning, Collaboration, and Documentation

The Fellow will also:

  • Facilitate peer-learning sessions among member organizations.
  • Represent Anthropocene Alliance within the SHIP coalition.
  • Share best practices across partner organizations.
  • Document successful advocacy approaches.
  • Monitor participation trends and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.
  • Strengthen collaboration across the national network.

Required Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Minimum of two years of professional experience in policy, advocacy, community organizing, or related fields.
  • Experience in environmental justice, climate action, or social justice initiatives is strongly preferred.
  • Knowledge of local and state policy-making processes.
  • Ability to convert complex policy issues into practical advocacy resources.
  • Strong communication and presentation skills.
  • Experience facilitating workshops and training sessions.
  • Excellent organizational and project management abilities.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Collaborative working style.
  • Self-motivation and accountability.
  • Passion for environmental justice and community empowerment.

Why Consider This Opportunity?

Working with Anthropocene Alliance offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including:

  • Contributing to meaningful environmental justice initiatives.
  • Supporting hundreds of grassroots organizations nationwide.
  • Working remotely from anywhere in the United States.
  • Collaborating with experts in climate policy and advocacy.
  • Building experience in national policy coordination.
  • Helping shape environmental policies affecting vulnerable communities.
  • Joining one of the country’s most influential environmental justice coalitions.

Employment Details

  • Position: Policy & Advocacy Fellow
  • Employment Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)
  • Work Location: Fully Remote
  • Eligible Location: Anywhere within the United States
  • Salary: $65,000 annually
  • Contract Duration: Two years (with potential for permanent employment)
  • Organization: Anthropocene Alliance (A2)

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit the following documents:

  • Updated Resume
  • Cover Letter

Applications should be emailed to:

Jobs@AnthropoceneAlliance.org

or visit the official website and apply

In your cover letter, be sure to mention where you learned about the position, as requested by the employer.

Applicants may also be asked to provide:

  • LinkedIn Profile URL
  • Current employer information
  • Earliest available start date

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been specified, so applicants are encouraged to apply early. 

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as the position may be filled once a suitable candidate has been identified.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Policy & Advocacy Fellow at Anthropocene Alliance (A2): Fully Remote U.S. Environmental Justice Job Paying $65,000 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office New Frontiers of Conflict Research 2026: Up to £200,000 Research Grant for Organizations and Consortia Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO), through its Global Research Technology Development (GRTD) portfolio, has launched an exciting new funding opportunity titled New Frontiers of Conflict Research. The initiative seeks innovative research that will strengthen the FCDO’s ability to better understand, anticipate, and respond to emerging global conflict trends. Organizations, universities, research institutions, […]

The post The UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office New Frontiers of Conflict Research 2026: Up to £200,000 Research Grant for Organizations and Consortia Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO), through its Global Research Technology Development (GRTD) portfolio, has launched an exciting new funding opportunity titled New Frontiers of Conflict Research. The initiative seeks innovative research that will strengthen the FCDO’s ability to better understand, anticipate, and respond to emerging global conflict trends.

Organizations, universities, research institutions, think tanks, and research consortia are invited to submit concept notes for research projects that address pressing conflict-related challenges across priority regions of the world.

This funding initiative represents an important opportunity for organizations with expertise in conflict research, peacebuilding, governance, international development, and policy analysis to contribute evidence that informs future global conflict responses.

Funding Overview

The New Frontiers of Conflict Research initiative is designed to commission short-term, high-quality research that provides practical insights into today’s rapidly changing conflict landscape.

Selected projects will contribute directly to strengthening policy development and evidence-based decision-making within the FCDO’s conflict research programme.

Applicants will compete for funding within one of two available budget categories:

  • £0 – £100,000
  • £100,000 – £200,000

Budget selection will be considered during the assessment process to ensure effective distribution of available funding across successful projects.

Purpose of the Programme

The initiative aims to improve understanding of emerging global conflicts by supporting research that:

  • Identifies new and evolving drivers of conflict.
  • Strengthens conflict prevention strategies.
  • Enhances policy responses to fragile environments.
  • Expands thematic and regional knowledge.
  • Produces evidence that supports future FCDO programming.
  • Generates practical recommendations for policymakers.

Ultimately, the programme seeks to build stronger evidence for preventing, managing, and responding to conflicts worldwide.

Who Can Apply?

Applications must be submitted by legally registered organizations.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Think tanks
  • Non-profit organizations
  • Policy research organizations
  • Consortia of legally registered organizations

Individual researchers cannot submit applications independently.

However, academics, consultants, and independent experts may participate as members of research teams led by eligible organizations.

FCDO’s Preferred Applicants

The programme particularly welcomes proposals that demonstrate diversity, inclusion, and equitable partnerships.

Applications are especially encouraged from organizations and consortia that include:

  • Researchers based in the Global South.
  • Institutions located in Low- and Middle-Income Countries (LMICs).
  • Researchers with first-hand experience working in fragile and conflict-affected environments.
  • Local organizations playing meaningful leadership roles.
  • Diverse multidisciplinary research teams.

Priority Geographic Areas

Research proposals should focus on at least one of the following regions:

  • Middle East
  • Horn of Africa
  • Eastern Europe, including Ukraine

Applicants may also submit comparative research involving other parts of the world, provided at least one of the priority regions above is included.

Research Themes

Concept notes should address one of the following major themes:

1. States, Societies and Citizens Under Stress

Research under this theme may examine:

  • Governance challenges
  • Political instability
  • Social cohesion
  • Public institutions
  • Community resilience
  • Citizen-state relations

2. The Shifting International Environment and New Forms of Intervention

Possible research areas include:

  • Emerging geopolitical dynamics
  • International cooperation
  • External actors in conflict settings
  • New intervention approaches
  • Changing global security trends

Two-Stage Application Process

The competition follows a two-stage commissioning process.

Stage One: Expression of Interest (Concept Note)

Applicants submit an initial concept note outlining their proposed research.

Stage Two: Full Proposal

Only shortlisted applicants will be invited to submit comprehensive research proposals.

Information Required in the Concept Note

Applicants will submit their concept notes through the SmartSimple application system.

The application requires:

  • Principal Investigator details
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Research questions
  • Geographic focus
  • Proposed methodology
  • Demonstration of feasibility within six months
  • Research team composition
  • Selected budget band

Assessment Criteria

Concept notes will undergo a competitive evaluation process.

Applications will be scored according to the following weighting:

  1. Strategic and Technical Fit – 35%
    • Alignment with FCDO priorities
    • Research relevance
    • Innovation
  2. Team Strength, Expertise and Equity – 35%
    • Team capability
    • Research experience
    • Inclusive partnerships
    • Leadership by local organizations
  3. Feasibility – 30%
    • Practicality of implementation
    • Realistic work plan
    • Deliverability within six months

Shortlisting Process

Following evaluation of concept notes, the FCDO will shortlist a limited number of applicants.

Shortlisting will consider:

  • Research quality
  • Policy relevance
  • Geographic balance
  • Thematic balance
  • Budget allocation
  • Overall programme priorities

Only shortlisted organizations will receive invitations to submit full proposals.

Project Timeline

The initiative follows the schedule below:

  • 11 June 2026: Expression of Interest opens
  • 30 June 2026 (23:00 BST): Application Deadline
  • 13–31 July 2026: Full proposal stage for shortlisted applicants
  • September 2026: Expected project commencement
  • February 2027: Submission of working papers
  • March 2027: Final revisions and project approval

Applicants should note that the timeline is indicative and may change.

New Frontiers Pathfinding Papers

The call builds upon three Pathfinding Papers developed through earlier consultations with researchers and stakeholders, particularly from Low- and Middle-Income Countries.

These publications explore:

  • Drivers of conflict
  • Pathways out of conflict
  • Knowledge production ecosystems in fragile and conflict-affected settings

Applicants are encouraged to review these papers to better align their research proposals with programme priorities.

Why You Should Apply

Successful applicants will have the opportunity to:

  • Secure research funding of up to £200,000.
  • Influence international conflict policy.
  • Collaborate with leading research organizations.
  • Strengthen partnerships with global institutions.
  • Produce high-impact policy research.
  • Expand research visibility internationally.
  • Contribute to peacebuilding and conflict prevention efforts.

Important Deadline

Expression of Interest (Concept Note) Deadline: 30 June 2026 at 23:00 BST

Late submissions are unlikely to be considered.

How to Apply

Eligible organizations must prepare their concept notes and submit applications through the SmartSimple application platform before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing early to allow sufficient time for developing strong research partnerships, refining methodologies, and completing all required documentation.

 Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post The UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office New Frontiers of Conflict Research 2026: Up to £200,000 Research Grant for Organizations and Consortia Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LEAP Africa Programmes Coordinator Recruitment 2026: Full-Time Opportunity in Lagos for Experienced Development Professionals
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
LEAP Africa is inviting qualified and experienced professionals to apply for the position of Programmes Coordinator under its MFAP Project. This exciting full-time opportunity offers development practitioners the chance to contribute to one of Africa’s leading youth-focused non-profit organizations dedicated to building dynamic, innovative, and principled leaders across the continent. The recruitment drive comes as […]
